title: API Gateway and Error Handling
description: Handling API requests, standardizing error responses, and serializing errors

actors.name: ExternalClient
actors.label: External Client (Browser / App)
actors.description: Sends HTTP API requests; receives structured error responses

actors.name: SDKUser
actors.label: SDK Developer
actors.description: Uses puter-js SDK; may enable APICallLogger for debugging

actors.name: InternalService
actors.label: Internal Service / Module
actors.description: Backend service that registers custom error codes and calls APIError.create()

actors.name: WebServer
actors.label: Web Server (Express app)
actors.description: Receives registered routes from PuterAPIService; dispatches requests

use_cases.name: Make API Request
use_cases.description: Client sends HTTP request to any registered endpoint (auth, kvstore, drivers, filesystem, etc.)

use_cases.name: Register API Routes
use_cases.description: PuterAPIService.__on_install.routes() registers all routers (apps, auth, kvstore, drivers, etc.) onto the Express app

use_cases.name: Create Standardized API Error
use_cases.description: Any service calls APIError.create(code, source, fields) to produce a typed error with status + message

use_cases.name: Register Custom Error Codes
use_cases.description: Service calls APIErrorService.register(codes) to add domain-specific error codes to the central registry

use_cases.name: Create Error via Service Registry
use_cases.description: APIErrorService.create(code, fields) looks up registered code and delegates to new APIError()

use_cases.name: Send Error HTTP Response
use_cases.description: APIError.write(res) sends res.status(status).send({ message, ...fields }) back to client

use_cases.name: Serialize Error to JSON
use_cases.description: APIError.serialize() returns { $: 'heyputer:api/APIError', message, status, ...fields } for transport or logging

use_cases.name: Convert Error to Query String
use_cases.description: APIError.querystringize(extra) encodes error fields as URL query parameters (for redirect error flows)

use_cases.name: Adapt Arbitrary Error to APIError
use_cases.description: APIError.adapt(err) wraps any non-APIError throwable into a standardized internal_error APIError

use_cases.name: Log API Call
use_cases.description: APICallLogger.logRequest({ service, operation, params, result, error }) logs SDK calls in format: service - operation - params - result

use_cases.name: Enable/Disable API Logging
use_cases.description: SDKUser calls APICallLogger.enable() / disable() / updateConfig() to toggle logging at runtime

use_cases.name: Check Logging Statistics
use_cases.description: APICallLogger.getStats() returns { enabled, config } for diagnostics

relationships: APIErrorService depends_on APIError (static codes initialized from APIError.codes)
PuterAPIService registers 30+ routers (auth, kvstore, drivers, filesystem, user mgmt)
APICallLogger is standalone (puter-js SDK, no backend dependency)
InternalServices call APIError.create() directly (50+ call sites across routers/services)

APIError: static create(status|code, source?, fields?) → APIError  [factory, handles string code or numeric status]
static adapt(err) → APIError  [wraps non-APIError as internal_error]
get message() → resolves fn or string
APICallLogger: logRequest({ service, operation, params, result, error })  [console.log/error]
PuterAPIService: async '__on_install.routes'()  [mounts all routers on Express app]

important_notes: APIError.codes is a large static map (~60+ codes); APIErrorService.codes starts as a copy and can be extended at runtime
PuterAPIService acts as the API gateway: single entry point that mounts all route handlers including auth, kvstore, drivers
APICallLogger lives in the client-side puter-js SDK (ES module, `export default`), not in backend
APIError.write() is the primary HTTP response mechanism for error cases in route handlers
APIError.serialize() is used for structured transport (e.g., driver call responses)
APIError.querystringize() supports redirect-based error flows (e.g., OAuth errors)
